# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 105

This is turn 105. Turns 1 through 104 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 105.

## Turn 105 — the simple-head determinant lemma also descends to the faithful image

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, the displayed exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is only its required verifier residue. 

Turn 104 separated the full tame frame from the faithful tame image in the local pure-determinant calculation. The adjacent simple-head determinant lemma requires the same separation. The corrected proof uses four distinct quotient levels:

\[
B=G/R,\qquad C=G/K,\qquad H=G/L,\qquad
H_V=\operatorname{im}(C\to\operatorname{GL}(V)).
\]

Here

\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=(K\cap S)/R,\qquad M/T_0\simeq V.
\]

Since \(K\le L\), there is a quotient map \(C\to H\). Its kernel \(L/K\) is a normal \(2\)-group, so it acts trivially on the simple characteristic-\(2\) module \(V\). Consequently,

\[
H_V=\operatorname{im}(C\to\operatorname{GL}(V))
    =\operatorname{im}(H\to\operatorname{GL}(V)).
\tag{105.1}
\]

The full group \(C\) must remain visible for extension classes, filtration-one edges, and inflated constants. Only the pure determinant calculation descends to \(H_V\).

### 1. The scalar pushout is naturally \(C\)-equivariant

Because \(R\le Z(K)\), the action of \(B=G/R\) on \(R\) factors through \(C=G/K\). Thus

\[
D=(R^\vee)^B=(R^\vee)^C.
\tag{105.2}
\]

For \(0\ne\lambda\in D\), the pushout

\[
K_\lambda=K/\ker\lambda
\]

is a \(C\)-equivariant central extension

\[
1\longrightarrow \mathbf F_2\longrightarrow K_\lambda
\longrightarrow M\longrightarrow 1,
\]

with square map \(q_\lambda:M\to\mathbf F_2\) and polar form
\(b_\lambda\).

The previously claimed reduction is

\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0,\qquad b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0,
\tag{105.3}
\]

followed by descent to a nonsingular quadratic form on \(V\). 

### 2. The polar form vanishes on the scalar radical

The adjoint map

\[
b_\lambda^\flat:M\longrightarrow M^\vee
\]

is \(C\)-linear. Since

\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}(M),
\qquad M/T_0\simeq V,
\]

we have

\[
\operatorname{soc}_C(M^\vee)\simeq V^\vee.
\]

Every composition factor of \(T_0\) is trivial, whereas \(V^\vee\) is nontrivial simple. Hence

\[
\operatorname{Hom}_C(T_0,M^\vee)=0,
\]

and therefore

\[
b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0.
\tag{105.4}
\]

### 3. A nonzero square on \(T_0\) would factor through \(H_V\)

Because of (105.4), the restriction \(q_\lambda|_{T_0}\) is a \(C\)-invariant linear functional. Suppose it is nonzero, and put

\[
T_1=\ker(q_\lambda|_{T_0}),\qquad
\widetilde M=M/T_1.
\]

Then there is an exact sequence of \(C\)-modules

\[
0\longrightarrow \mathbf F_2t
\longrightarrow \widetilde M
\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow 0,
\tag{105.5}
\]

where \(t\) lies in the polar radical and

\[
q_\lambda(t)=1.
\tag{105.6}
\]

Let

\[
N_V=\ker(C\to H_V).
\]

It acts trivially on \(V\) and on \(\mathbf F_2t\). Thus, for \(n\in N_V\), its possible action on \(\widetilde M\) has the shear form

\[
n\cdot m=m+f_n(\bar m)t
\]

for some \(f_n\in V^\vee\). Quadratic invariance gives

\[
q_\lambda(nm)
 =q_\lambda(m+f_n(\bar m)t)
 =q_\lambda(m)+f_n(\bar m),
\]

using \(b_\lambda(t,\widetilde M)=0\) and \(q_\lambda(t)=1\). Hence \(f_n=0\). Therefore \(N_V\) acts trivially on \(\widetilde M\), and (105.5) factors through \(H_V\), not merely through the larger tame quotient \(H\).

Now

\[
H^1(H_V,V^\vee)=0.
\tag{105.7}
\]

Indeed:

- if \(V\) is ramified, its odd inertia subgroup \(I_V\) has
  \((V^\vee)^{I_V}=0\); since \(|I_V|\) is odd, the
  Hochschild–Serre spectral sequence gives
  \(H^*(H_V,V^\vee)=0\);
- if \(V\) is unramified, its faithful image \(H_V\) is cyclic of odd
  order, so Maschke exactness again gives \(H^1(H_V,V^\vee)=0\).

Thus

\[
\operatorname{Ext}^1_{\mathbf F_2[H_V]}(V,\mathbf F_2)=0,
\]

so (105.5) splits. Its projection onto \(\mathbf F_2t\) yields a nonzero
\(C\)-map \(M\to\mathbf F_2\), contradicting

\[
(M^\vee)^C=0.
\]

Therefore

\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0.
\tag{105.8}
\]

This is the precise faithful-image repair of the earlier sentence that the quotient “factors through the tame quotient.”

### 4. The descended form is nonzero and nonsingular

Equations (105.4) and (105.8) give a \(C\)-invariant quadratic form

\[
\bar q_\lambda:V\longrightarrow\mathbf F_2.
\]

It is nonzero. Otherwise \(q_\lambda=b_\lambda=0\), so \(K_\lambda\) would be elementary abelian. But \(\ker\lambda\le R=\Phi(K)\), and therefore

\[
\Phi(K_\lambda)=R/\ker\lambda\ne1,
\]

a contradiction.

The radical of the polar form of \(\bar q_\lambda\) is an \(H_V\)-submodule of the simple module \(V\). If it were all of \(V\), then \(\bar q_\lambda\) would be an \(H_V\)-invariant linear functional, impossible for nontrivial simple \(V\). Hence \(\bar q_\lambda\) is nonsingular.

Thus every nonzero Fourier pushout is genuinely a determinant pushout of the faithful simple head \(V\).

### 5. Transgression and shearing remain over the full group \(C\)

This is where the quotient labels must not be collapsed to \(H_V\).

Let

\[
\kappa_\lambda\in H^2(B,\mathbf F_2)
\]

be the central-cover class. If its \(T_0\)-filtration edge is nonzero, finite-Heisenberg deformation duality gives the uniform half-split already used in Turn 103.

If that edge vanishes, then \(\kappa_\lambda\) descends through

\[
\bar B=B/T_0.
\]

There is an extension

\[
1\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow\bar B
\longrightarrow C\longrightarrow1
\tag{105.9}
\]

with class \(\eta\in H^2(C,V)\). Since \(\bar q_\lambda\) survives in
\(H^2(\bar B,\mathbf F_2)\), its Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre transgression vanishes:

\[
0=d_2(\bar q_\lambda)
  =\bar b_\lambda^\flat{}_*(\eta).
\tag{105.10}
\]

Because \(\bar b_\lambda^\flat:V\to V^\vee\) is a \(C\)-module isomorphism, (105.10) implies

\[
\eta=0.
\]

Therefore (105.9) splits **over the full group \(C\)**:

\[
\bar B\simeq V\rtimes C.
\tag{105.11}
\]

Likewise, the marking-preserving shear is a \(C\)-equivariant operation. A residual edge

\[
\gamma\in H^1(C,V^\vee)
\]

corresponds via \(\bar b_\lambda^\flat\) to some
\(a\in H^1(C,V)\); the automorphism

\[
(v,c)\longmapsto(v+a(c),c)
\]

removes \(\gamma\) and changes only the inflated class

\[
\delta\in H^2(C,\mathbf F_2).
\]

Only after these full-\(C\) inflated and filtration-one components have been separated does the remaining pure determinant calculation descend to \(H_V\), exactly as established in Turn 104. 

The corrected division of labor is therefore:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{object} & \text{group that must remain visible}\\ \hline
\text{outer exact-image tame frame} & H=G/L\\
\text{elementary extension, transgression, shear, inflated class}
   & C=G/K\\
\text{current central cover and Fourier obstruction} & B=G/R\\
\text{pure determinant zero count} & H_V=\operatorname{im}(C\to\mathrm{GL}(V)).
\end{array}
\]

No candidate correction is needed.

**ESTABLISHED**: Reproved the simple-head determinant lemma with all quotient labels separated. The square map vanishes on the scalar radical because any hypothetical nonzero restriction forces the resulting module extension to factor through the faithful image \(H_V\), where \(H^1(H_V,V^\vee)=0\). The descended form on \(V\) is nonzero and nonsingular. Transgression and shearing remain statements over the full quotient \(C=G/K\); only the final pure-determinant calculation descends to \(H_V\).

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The consolidated proof still needs an independent line-by-line manuscript audit, but the faithful-versus-full quotient ambiguity in both determinant interfaces is now removed.

**NEXT**: Replace the simple-head paragraph surrounding (103.13)–(103.16) by this four-level \(B/C/H/H_V\) proposition, then audit Sections 3–5 of Turn 103 once more for consistent quotient labels before producing the standalone submission manuscript.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
